name: Uptake Technologies
summary: Chicago-based industrial predictive-maintenance and analytics platform founded by Brad Keywell partnered with Caterpillar; its valuation crashed from a reported $2.3B peak amid repeated rounds of layoffs.
lesson: Being first to a hyped category doesn't guarantee a defensible business if enterprise ROI stays murky.

name: Petuum Inc
summary: CMU professor Eric Xing's SoftBank-backed AI platform, including industrial and manufacturing automation offerings, shut down after burning through funding amid reports of founders struggling to run the business.
lesson: Research pedigree doesn't substitute for operating discipline.

name: Element Analytics
summary: Oakland industrial IT/OT data-unification platform (Element Unify) raised a 2020 Series B but is no longer active.
lesson: Data-integration plays are hard to monetize standalone when platform incumbents bundle 'good enough' integration for free.

name: Innovbot
summary: UK low-cost wireless vibration-sensor predictive-maintenance startup ceased operating after failing to scale past pilots.
lesson: Cheap hardware sensors aren't a moat without a defensible software/data layer.

name: Symbio Robotics
summary: AI software for real-time control of industrial robots used by Ford and Toyota; closed its Emeryville facility and liquidated assets in 2022.
lesson: Selling into legacy automotive robot cells means enterprise sales cycles that can outlast your runway.

name: Attabotics
summary: Calgary 3D robotic warehouse and goods-to-person automation firm filed bankruptcy in 2025 after revenue fell from $11.4M in 2022 to $3M in 2024 and a Series D stalled.
lesson: Capital-intensive robotics needs a bigger margin of safety before demand cyclicality hits.

name: Soft Robotics Inc
summary: Robot-gripper and vision AI maker sold its gripper product line to Schmalz in 2024 and spun its AI/vision technology into a new company, Oxipital AI, after its CEO said grippers were too niche a business.
lesson: A narrow hardware niche may not support a standalone venture-scale company.

name: 6 River Systems
summary: AI warehouse robot maker (Chuck) that Shopify bought for $450M in 2019 was sold to Ocado for just $12.7M in 2023 after Shopify abandoned its logistics ambitions.
lesson: Diligence the acquirer's strategic staying power, not just the product.

name: Zymergen
summary: Used AI/ML and lab automation to optimize microbial strain engineering for materials manufacturing; IPO'd at roughly $3B valuation, then collapsed after product and disclosure failures and was acquired by Ginkgo Bioworks.
lesson: AI-driven 'lights-out manufacturing' hype can mask a product that doesn't work at commercial scale.

name: Osaro
summary: Deep reinforcement-learning robotics company pivoted from general robot perception to warehouse piece-picking; after a decade remains an ~85-person company relying on debt financing.
lesson: A decade of runway isn't proof of PMF if headcount and revenue never scale.

name: READY Robotics
summary: Maker of ForgeOS, a no-code operating system for programming factory robots, shut down abruptly when a follow-on round fell through; its IP was later acquired by Standard Bots.
lesson: Never let survival hinge on a single funding round closing.

name: Dorabot
summary: Shenzhen computer-vision and deep-learning robotics firm for automated loading, sorting, and induction ceased operations amid the 2024 robotics funding downturn.
lesson: Early-mover status doesn't protect you once better-funded rivals and a downturn hit together.

name: Dextrous Robotics
summary: Chopstick-like AI robot for automated trailer unloading dissolved after failing to close a follow-on round amid intense competition from Boston Dynamics, Dexterity, and Pickle Robot.
lesson: Technical capability isn't enough with four or five well-funded rivals chasing the same niche.

name: Zume Inc
summary: Started as AI/robotics pizza manufacturing, then pivoted to AI-optimized sustainable packaging manufacturing; burned through capital including $375M from SoftBank before shutting down.
lesson: Mega-capital can fund a pivot but can't manufacture a market that isn't there.

name: Karakuri
summary: UK AI-driven automated meal-assembly line maker (FRYR, SEMBLR) for food manufacturers closed after funding talks and an acquisition by Henny Penny fell through.
lesson: A strategic investor's minority stake isn't a committed exit.

name: Seebo
summary: Israeli industrial AI company pivoted from consumer IoT to manufacturing quality and downtime prediction; workforce shrank from 50 to 35 during the pivot with little visible momentum since its 2021 raise.
lesson: A pivot needs accelerating traction within a couple years or assume slow bleed.

name: Prophesee
summary: French neuromorphic event-based vision-sensor company used in industrial machine vision and inspection filed insolvency in mid-2024; its technology was partly absorbed via an AMD licensing deal.
lesson: Novel sensor hardware needs one paying beachhead market, not three at once.

name: BionicHIVE
summary: Israeli warehouse and logistics robotics coordination-AI firm shut down as the Israel war disrupted the economy and cut off foreign investor access.
lesson: Macro and geopolitical shocks can kill a viable startup by severing capital access.

name: Kivnon Logistica
summary: Spanish AI-guided AGV maker for factory material handling, heavily exposed to automotive manufacturing, shut down after a loss on revenue as automotive demand weakened.
lesson: Concentration in one cyclical end-market can sink a revenue-generating industrial vendor.

name: Voodoo Manufacturing
summary: Brooklyn on-demand 3D-printing factory using software to orchestrate a 200+ printer farm shut down permanently during COVID after high fixed costs made it unsustainable.
lesson: Thin-margin manufacturing-as-a-service has little room for a demand shock.

name: C3.ai
summary: Enterprise industrial-AI platform for predictive maintenance and asset optimization that IPO'd on the NYSE in December 2020 and serves oil & gas, manufacturing, and aerospace/defense customers.
lesson: A broad enterprise AI platform can reach public-market scale if it locks in large industrial accounts early.

name: Cognite
summary: Norwegian industrial-data and AI platform spun out of Aker ASA that grew into a well-funded, fast-growing independent company valued above $1.6B.
lesson: A corporate spinout with deep industrial-domain access can outcompete generalist data platforms.

name: Sight Machine
summary: Manufacturing analytics and AI platform standardizing shop-floor data for GM, Fiat Chrysler, and other large manufacturers.
lesson: Standardizing messy factory data into a common model is a durable wedge into large manufacturers.

name: Landing AI
summary: Andrew Ng's computer-vision platform (LandingLens) helps manufacturers build defect-detection models with small labeled datasets.
lesson: Founder credibility plus a genuinely differentiated technical approach (small-data CV) can open enterprise doors fast.

name: Drishti Technologies
summary: AI video-analytics company for manual assembly lines, used by Ford, Toyota, Denso, and Continental, was acquired by Apple in June 2023.
lesson: A tight vertical focus on manual-assembly analytics can make a company an attractive acquisition target.

name: Senseye
summary: UK predictive-maintenance and asset-intelligence software company was acquired by Siemens after a multi-year MindSphere partnership.
lesson: A long integration partnership with a large industrial platform can convert into an acquisition.

name: Presenso
summary: Israeli AI predictive-maintenance software company was acquired by SKF in March 2023.
lesson: Bearing and rotating-equipment incumbents value AI predictive-maintenance IP enough to acquire outright.

name: Poka
summary: Quebec 'connected worker' platform used by Nestle and Mars was acquired by IFS for roughly $200M.
lesson: Frontline-worker software with strong retention in CPG manufacturing commands a premium acquisition price.

name: Kalypso
summary: Industry 4.0 digital-transformation consultancy focused on manufacturers was acquired by Rockwell Automation in May 2020.
lesson: Consultancies with deep vertical expertise are a low-risk way for industrial incumbents to acquire capability.

name: Falkonry
summary: Industrial time-series anomaly-detection AI used by the U.S. Navy and large manufacturers was acquired by IFS in September 2023.
lesson: Capital-efficient time-series AI companies can reach a strong exit without mega-rounds.

name: Amper Technologies
summary: Manufacturing execution and analytics platform (FactoryOS) was acquired by ECI Software Solutions in December 2025.
lesson: Shop-floor analytics platforms remain acquisition targets for software roll-ups serving SMB manufacturers.

name: Rebellion Photonics
summary: AI visual gas-leak detection company for oil & gas and petrochemical plants was acquired by Honeywell in December 2019.
lesson: Safety-critical AI sensing with clear regulatory and cost-avoidance value sells well to industrial conglomerates.

name: FogHorn Systems
summary: Edge-AI software company for industrial and building IoT was acquired by Johnson Controls in January 2022.
lesson: Edge-AI infrastructure plays get absorbed by building/industrial automation giants seeking software depth.

name: Inspekto
summary: 'Inspection-in-a-box' AI visual quality-control system used by Bosch, BMW, and Toyota was acquired by Siemens in 2024.
lesson: Turnkey AI inspection hardware that removes integration friction is attractive to automation incumbents.

name: Path Robotics
summary: Autonomous robotic welding company (AW-3, AF-1) raised large ongoing rounds including a $100M Series D in October 2024.
lesson: Solving a severe skilled-labor shortage (welding) with AI robotics attracts sustained large-scale capital.

name: Nanotronics
summary: Combines computer vision, AI, and optical microscopy for defect inspection and process control in aerospace, electronics, and semiconductor manufacturing.
lesson: Deep-tech inspection platforms serving high-value semiconductor and aerospace lines can sustain long independent growth.

name: Vecna Robotics
summary: AI autonomous forklifts and material-handling robots company raised a $100M Series C in 2024 and hired a new CEO from Motional.
lesson: Autonomous heavy material-handling equipment can command large late-stage rounds when unit economics prove out.

name: Formic
summary: Robots-as-a-service subscription model for manufacturers has logged over 100,000 production hours at 99%+ uptime.
lesson: A subscription/RaaS pricing model lowers the adoption barrier for capital-constrained manufacturers.

name: Covariant
summary: Robotics foundation-model company had its founders and about a quarter of its staff hired by Amazon, which also licensed its technology, in a 'reverse acquihire' in August 2024, while Covariant continued operating independently.
lesson: A partial acqui-hire/licensing deal can monetize foundational robotics AI without a full company sale.

name: Wandelbots
summary: No-code robot-programming platform (TracePen) lets factory workers without coding skills program industrial robots.
lesson: Removing the programming skill barrier for industrial robots expands the addressable buyer base.

name: Elementum
summary: AI supply-chain and manufacturing-operations visibility platform spun out of Flex.
lesson: A corporate spinout with an existing manufacturing customer base can bootstrap product-market fit quickly.

name: Retrocausal
summary: 'Kaizen Copilot' generative-AI assembly-line coaching tool reports up to 25% higher first-time yield for customers.
lesson: A narrowly scoped, measurable ROI tool can grow efficiently on modest funding.

name: Chooch AI
summary: Computer-vision platform serving manufacturing, defense, and safety detection and anomaly use cases.
lesson: Horizontal computer-vision platforms can find traction across multiple industrial verticals at once.

name: Overview.ai
summary: AI visual-inspection and defect-detection platform for manufacturers continues to operate serving industrial quality-control customers.
lesson: A focused defect-detection product can sustain a modestly funded, steady business.

name: Elementary Robotics
summary: AI visual quality-inspection hardware and software company raised a $30M Series B in 2022.
lesson: Combining purpose-built hardware with AI inspection software differentiates against pure-software competitors.

name: Canvass AI
summary: No-code industrial AI platform backed by Yamaha Motor Ventures and Google's Gradient Ventures.
lesson: Strategic corporate investors can validate and open enterprise sales channels for industrial AI startups.

name: Petasense
summary: IIoT vibration-monitoring and predictive-maintenance platform for rotating industrial equipment.
lesson: Focused sensor-plus-software offerings for a specific asset class (rotating equipment) can sustain a durable niche business.

name: Aquant
summary: AI platform mining service and maintenance data along with technician knowledge to guide equipment-repair decisions.
lesson: Capturing tacit technician knowledge as structured AI data creates a hard-to-replicate asset.

name: Nanoprecise Sci Corp
summary: AI 'Energy-Centered Maintenance' platform for industrial motors closed an oversubscribed $38M Series C up-round in March 2025 at a valuation above $100M.
lesson: Tying predictive maintenance to energy savings, not just uptime, broadens the ROI pitch to industrial buyers.

name: Konux
summary: AI and IIoT predictive-maintenance platform for railway infrastructure, with Deutsche Bahn as a flagship customer.
lesson: Landing one large, prestigious anchor customer can validate an industrial AI platform for years of expansion.

name: Bit Stew Systems
summary: Industrial data-integration platform (MIx Core) was acquired by GE Digital for approximately $153M to strengthen the Predix platform.
lesson: Data-integration middleware can become a strategic acquisition when a larger platform needs the capability fast.

name: Kinema Systems
summary: Deep-learning vision company for robotic bin-picking and depalletizing was acquired by Boston Dynamics in April 2019.
lesson: Perception software for logistics robotics is a natural tuck-in acquisition for hardware robotics leaders.

name: Ambi Robotics
summary: AI parcel-sorting robotic arm company (AmbiSort) had sorted over 100 million packages by 2024.
lesson: Demonstrated volume-scale throughput is a powerful proof point for AI robotics sales cycles.

name: RightHand Robotics
summary: AI-guided robotic piece-picking company (RightPick) raised a 2025 round led by Rockwell Automation.
lesson: A strategic lead investor from industrial automation signals durable enterprise distribution.

name: Plus One Robotics
summary: AI vision software for parcel-handling and logistics robots raised a $50M Series C in 2023.
lesson: Software-only AI vision layered on third-party robot arms can scale capital-efficiently.

name: Locus Robotics
summary: AI/software-driven warehouse robotics company that filed Chapter 11 in Feb 2023 as post-pandemic e-commerce demand normalized after a huge valuation run-up; restructured and continues at smaller scale.
lesson: Valuations built on pandemic-era extrapolation can evaporate fast; stress-test growth assumptions against a demand pullback.

name: Castor Technologies
summary: Israeli software analyzing CAD files to recommend additive- vs traditional-manufacturing decisions; filed for court-ordered liquidation after a 2023+ industry-wide pullback in 3D-printing R&D budgets dried up its pipeline.
lesson: A software layer riding on customers' own R&D budgets is exposed when that end market's capex contracts.

name: Picnic Works
summary: Seattle robotics company whose AI-guided Picnic Pizza Station let one worker make ~100 customized pizzas/hour for stadiums and high-volume venues; ceased operations after struggling to close a follow-on round.
lesson: A decade of iteration and rebrands without durable unit economics is a warning sign, not resilience.

name: Chowbotics
summary: Made 'Sally,' a robotic kiosk assembling fresh salads/bowls from ~22 ingredients, deployed in hospitals, campuses and grocers; DoorDash shut the unit down 18 months after acquiring it in 2021 after it missed internal performance benchmarks.
lesson: Acquisition is not an exit for a startup's mission; a strategic acquirer can kill the product fast if it doesn't fit the parent's roadmap.

name: Sarcos Technology and Robotics
summary: Public industrial robotics/exoskeleton company (Nasdaq: STRC) that burned ~$83M in the year to Sept 2023 against minimal revenue; cut ~25% of staff and relaunched as software-only 'Palladyne AI' in 2024.
lesson: A SPAC-funded hardware robotics story with minimal revenue can burn nine figures fast; a late pivot to software is usually a survival move.

name: German Bionic
summary: Maker of AI-powered 'Apogee' powered industrial exoskeletons for warehouse/logistics workers; filed for bankruptcy despite reportedly having a full order book.
lesson: A full order book doesn't guarantee survival if working-capital financing for hardware production dries up faster than orders convert to cash.

name: Markforged
summary: Industrial 3D-printing/composite-manufacturing company SPAC'd at ~$2.1B in 2021; revenue declined and cash nearly halved through 2024, compounded by a $17.3M patent-infringement judgment, before being acquired for just $116M in 2025.
lesson: SPAC-era hardware-manufacturer valuations were often disconnected from unit economics.

name: Fathom Digital Manufacturing
summary: On-demand digital manufacturing (3D printing, CNC, injection molding) roll-up platform that went public via SPAC at $1.5B in 2021; revenue kept declining and it was taken private at a steep discount in 2024.
lesson: SPAC roll-ups of several smaller manufacturers can mask weak organic growth.

name: Cyngn
summary: Public developer of autonomous-driving software (DriveMod) for industrial vehicles like forklifts and tuggers; despite $132M raised, market cap has collapsed to ~$17M.
lesson: A market cap far below lifetime capital raised is one of the clearest signals of a broken venture bet, even without a formal shutdown.

name: Noodle.ai
summary: Supply-chain/manufacturing 'FlowOps' AI platform backed by ServiceNow, Honeywell and TPG; despite raising over $100M, it rebranded to 'Daybreak' and raised a fresh round framed as an early Series A.
lesson: A rebrand paired with a reset round labeled as early-stage despite prior nine-figure raises usually signals a down round or restructuring.

name: Takeoff Technologies
summary: Grocery micro-fulfillment robotics/software company that filed Chapter 11 after revenue fell 33% to $27.3M, with two customers making up over two-thirds of revenue; assets sold to Woolworths for just $2.5M.
lesson: Customer concentration turns a capital-intensive robotics business into an existential risk the moment either relationship stalls.

name: Fabric
summary: Israeli/US warehouse micro-fulfillment robotics (ASRS) company that laid off 40% of ~300 staff in 2022 while pivoting from an operated-service model to a software/hardware platform model, then cut another 15% in 2024.
lesson: Repeated years of deep layoffs plus a fundamental business-model pivot signal a company still searching for product-market fit.
